info.name: LMS — Announcements (v1)
info.description:
Mobile app announcement APIs.

Audience rules (driven by the logged-in user's role + the announcement `type` set in admin):
- `teacher` announcements → shown to **teachers only**
- `user` (student) announcements → shown to **students only**
- `all` (both) announcements → shown to **everyone**

The server scopes results automatically by the authenticated user's role, so the app does not need to send anything to get the correct list. Teachers only ever receive `teacher` + `all`; students only ever receive `user` + `all`.

Auth: Bearer token (Sanctum). Set the `authToken` environment variable after login.

request.description:
Returns the last 30 announcements (last 30 days) for the user's organization, already scoped to what the logged-in role may see.

The `type` field in the body is OPTIONAL and only narrows the list within the allowed set (useful for app tabs):
- omit it → everything the role can see (recommended default)
- `"both"` (or `"all"`) → only the both-audience announcements
- `"student"` (or `"user"`) → only student announcements (students/admins)
- `"teacher"` → only teacher announcements (teachers/admins)

A value the role isn't allowed to see is ignored (the role scope still applies), so a student can never pull teacher-only announcements.

Each item includes the creator ("Posted By") fields: `creator_name`, `creator_email`, and `creator_avatar` (full URL of the creator's profile image, falling back to the school/organization logo, or `null` when neither is set). File fields `image_url` and `pdf_url` are full URLs (or `null`).

Fetch a single announcement by id. Returns 403 if the announcement's audience type is not visible to the logged-in user's role (e.g. a student opening a teacher-only announcement).

Response includes the creator ("Posted By") fields: `creator_name`, `creator_email`, and `creator_avatar` (full URL of the creator's profile image, falling back to the school/organization logo, or `null` when neither is set), plus `image_url` and `pdf_url` (full URLs, or `null`).
